page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4a server family. It features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core, aligning with standard general-purpose resource distributions.

This server type does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the n4a-standard-64 is designed for general-purpose workloads. It offers resource density suitable for multi-threaded applications, enterprise web serving, application servers, and moderate database deployments. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances, providing predictable cost-to-performance efficiency for sustained enterprise workloads.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-64 is a general-purpose server instance configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM on the x86_64 architecture. This configuration delivers a balanced 1:4 ratio of virtual processing cores to memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Designed for predictable performance, its dedicated CPU allocation makes it suitable for sustained workloads. The n4a-standard-64 is positioned for enterprise applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and medium-scale database workloads that require significant multi-core processing capabilities without specialized memory or acceleration needs.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-64 is an x86_64 general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, designed for balanced enterprise workloads and multi-threaded applications.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-standard-64 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, offering a balanced 1:4 resource ratio.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of memory for sustained enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
No integrated GPU or local storage included
Dedicated CPU allocation for predictable performance
Suitable for enterprise web serving, application servers, and databases
